title: Fix Console prediction execute_tactus contract
description:
As a Console user, I want prediction requests to run once item, score, and scorecard are known, so that the assistant does not fail on its own documented execute_tactus example.

Feature: Console score prediction

Scenario: Run prediction with resolved identifiers
Given a Console conversation has an item id, scorecard identifier, and score identifier
When the assistant calls plexus.score.predict through execute_tactus
Then the call uses scorecard_identifier and score_identifier and returns a structured prediction result.

Scenario: Missing prediction context is actionable
Given a prediction request lacks required score or scorecard context
When execute_tactus validates the request
Then it returns a structured error naming scorecard_identifier and score_identifier.

Definition of Done:
- Runtime score prediction accepts the canonical identifier argument names.
- Console prompt teaches the canonical prediction recipe and no repeated confirmation once identifiers are known.
- Runtime account context is used for item resolution.
- Focused tests and the failing-session smoke call pass.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented canonical prediction contract in runtime and Console prompt. Focused tests pass, and real smoke call for PolicyPoint - Non-Sales / Acknowledges Before Redirecting / item 311432364 returned ok=true via plexus.score.predict with scorecard_identifier and score_identifier.
